papers.title: Proof of Useful Intelligence (PoUI): Blockchain Consensus Beyond Energy Waste
papers.authors: Chong, Z.-K., Ohsaki, H., Ng, B.
papers.abstract_summary: Proposes PoUI as a replacement for PoW, where validators prove useful AI computation instead of hash puzzles.
papers.key_contribution: PoUI consensus mechanism definition and motivation

papers.title: A Proof of Useful Work for Artificial Intelligence on the Blockchain
papers.abstract_summary: Early work coupling AI task completion as proof-of-work; introduces concept of AI-native consensus.

papers.title: AI-Powered Consensus Mechanisms in Blockchain
papers.abstract_summary: Survey of AI integration in blockchain consensus, covering reputation systems and AI-based validator selection.
papers.key_contribution: AI consensus survey and gap identification

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence for Web 3.0: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.abstract_summary: Broad survey of AI-Web3 intersection, including AI in DeFi, NFTs, and blockchain governance.

papers.title: Bittensor: A Peer-to-Peer Intelligence Market
papers.abstract_summary: Industry AI-native blockchain where validators earn by contributing AI compute tasks.

papers.title: Lightchain AI: Protocol for Decentralized AI Computation
papers.abstract_summary: Layer-1 blockchain natively designed for AI inference as first-class consensus primitive.

papers.title: Coin.AI: A Proof-of-Useful-Work Scheme for Blockchain-Based Distributed Deep Learning
papers.abstract_summary: Introduces Coin.AI, a framework for distributed deep learning consensus where blocks are mined when training hits accuracy thresholds.

papers.title: Decentralized Deep Learning Training on Blockchain with Proof of Useful Work
papers.abstract_summary: Presents a verifiable training protocol with random validation epochs to prevent lazy miner shortcuts.

papers.title: Proof of Federated Learning (PoFL): Consensus for Sustainable Blockchain
papers.abstract_summary: Integrates federated learning into consensus, utilizing model aggregation steps as validator selection metrics.

papers.title: DeepChain: Auditable and Privacy-Preserving Deep Learning with Blockchain
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
papers.abstract_summary: Foundational framework for decentralized AI model training with incentive mechanisms and cryptographic verification.

papers.title: Proof of Training (PoT): Verifiable Machine Learning on Distributed Ledgers
papers.venue: Journal of Network and Computer Applications
papers.abstract_summary: Formulates cryptographic proofs for verifiable neural network training over untrusted peer-to-peer networks.

papers.title: A Reputation-Based PoUI Consensus Mechanism for Edge Intelligence
papers.abstract_summary: Proposes trust-based node score updates utilizing inference latency and accuracy profiles for resource-constrained edge systems.

papers.title: Incentivizing Useful Work in Blockchains: A Game-Theoretic Analysis
papers.abstract_summary: Examines game-theoretic properties of PoUW, showing security bounds when the computational task has commercial utility.

papers.title: Useful Proof of Work Consensus for Machine Learning Inference
papers.abstract_summary: Leverages large-scale ML inference tasks to compute consensus validator rankings, optimizing network throughput.

papers.title: Decentralized AI Governance via Blockchain-Backed Consensus
papers.abstract_summary: Develops a consensus protocol with model performance metrics embedded in validator election algorithms.

papers.title: Trust-Score Models in Blockchain Consensus: A Systematic Survey
papers.abstract_summary: Reviews historical reputation-based consensus protocols and identifies vulnerabilities to strategic collusive attackers.

papers.title: A Survey of Zero-Knowledge Proof Based Verifiable Machine Learning
papers.abstract_summary: Comprehensive ZKML survey covering 27 studies. Identifies proving cost, circuit expressiveness, and deployment complexity as key bottlenecks.

papers.title: Automated Detection of Underconstrained Circuits for Zero-Knowledge Proofs
papers.abstract_summary: Formalizes underconstrained circuit problem; builds automated detection tool. Foundational to our ZKML taxonomy.

papers.title: On-Chain Zero-Knowledge Machine Learning: An Overview and Comparison
papers.abstract_summary: Compares on-chain ZKML systems including EZKL, Modulus Labs, and zkML frameworks.

papers.title: ZER0N: An AI-Assisted Vulnerability Discovery and Blockchain-Backed Integrity Framework
papers.abstract_summary: Combines AI and blockchain for vulnerability discovery; intersects ZKML and blockchain security.

papers.title: ZKML: Verifiable Machine Learning using Zero-Knowledge Proof
papers.abstract_summary: Industry technical report on ZKML production deployment challenges and security considerations.

papers.title: Modulus Labs: On-Chain AI Verification at Scale
papers.abstract_summary: Demonstrates on-chain verification of ML models with up to 18 million parameters using ZKML.

papers.title: EZKL: Making ZKML Accessible
papers.abstract_summary: EZKL framework for compiling neural networks to ZK circuits. Core tooling for our RQ2 analysis.

papers.title: Worldcoin: A Privacy-Preserving Proof of Personhood Protocol
papers.abstract_summary: Uses ZKML for iris-based identity verification on-chain. Production ZKML deployment case study.

papers.title: ZKML: An Optimizing System for Verifiable Machine Learning Inference
papers.abstract_summary: Optimizes ZKML systems, proposing layout-aware constraints for deep convolutional networks, reducing proving overhead.

papers.title: Engineering Trustworthy Machine-Learning Operations with Zero-Knowledge Proofs
papers.abstract_summary: Evaluates ZKMLOps pipelines, identifying threat models around out-of-bounds inputs and underconstrained floating-point conversions.

papers.title: Vulnerability Analysis of Halo2-Based ZKML Circuits
papers.abstract_summary: Exposes logic bugs in Halo2 lookup tables for activation functions like ReLU and Sigmoid, causing verification bypasses.

papers.title: On the Formal Verification of ZK-SNARK Circuits for Neural Network Inference
papers.abstract_summary: Formulates deductive verification rules for ZKML circuits, proving correctness bounds of quantized model weights.

papers.title: Soundness Violations in Quantized ZKML Systems
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
papers.abstract_summary: Demonstrates how quantization scaling factor range mismatches allow invalid proofs to be verified as authentic.

papers.title: Underconstrained Circuit Detection in Zero-Knowledge Cryptography
papers.abstract_summary: Builds static analysis tools for Circom and Halo2 to detect missing constraint violations in variable-length matrix operations.

papers.title: vCNN: Verifiable Convolutional Neural Networks using Zero-Knowledge Proofs
papers.abstract_summary: First zero-knowledge proof framework optimized for CNN operations, demonstrating secure matrix transformations.

papers.title: Verifying the Integrity of Deep Neural Networks with SNARKs
papers.abstract_summary: Analyzes the computational limits of verifying deep layer representations in zero knowledge, suggesting partition proofs.

papers.title: Security Audits of Production ZKML Deployments: A Multi-Case Study
papers.abstract_summary: Audits commercial ZKML implementations, highlighting common vulnerabilities including unconstrained inputs and state mismatch.

papers.title: zk-MNIST: Verifiable MNIST Inference with Zero-Knowledge Proofs
papers.abstract_summary: A benchmark implementation of MNIST classification inside a Groth16 circuit, analyzing proving times and soundness gates.

papers.title: Quantum Disruption: An SoK of How Post-Quantum Attackers Reshape Blockchain Security and Performance
papers.abstract_summary: Comprehensive SoK covering quantum attacks across UTXO, account-balance models, and consensus. Shows PQC swap introduces intolerable overhead naively.
papers.key_contribution: PQC blockchain threat landscape and overhead baseline

papers.title: NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards: ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204)
papers.abstract_summary: Official FIPS 203 and 204 standards finalizing ML-KEM (Kyber) and ML-DSA (Dilithium). Core reference for our benchmarks.

papers.title: Assessing the Impact of Post-Quantum Digital Signature Algorithms on Blockchains
papers.abstract_summary: Direct benchmark study of PQC DSA overhead on blockchain. Closest prior work to our RQ3.
papers.key_contribution: PQC blockchain overhead assessment (closest prior RQ3 work)

papers.title: Blockchain in the Quantum Era: Surveying Security Challenges and Post-Quantum Cryptography
papers.abstract_summary: Survey of quantum threats to blockchain and PQC migration strategies.

papers.title: Post-Quantum Distributed Ledger Technology: A Systematic Survey
papers.abstract_summary: Systematic survey of PQC integration in DLT systems.

papers.title: Blockchain Security Risk Assessment in Quantum Era, Migration Strategies and Proactive Defense
papers.abstract_summary: Risk assessment framework for blockchain quantum migration; proposes phased PQC deployment.

papers.title: QUASAR: Achieving Quantum Readiness for PQC on RISC-V at Minimum Hardware Cost
papers.abstract_summary: Hardware-accelerated PQC implementation on RISC-V; relevant to our M4 mitigation.

papers.title: Toward Quantum-Safe 6G: Experimental Evaluation of PQC Techniques
papers.abstract_summary: Real-world PQC performance benchmarks in high-throughput network environments.
papers.key_contribution: PQC performance in high-TPS networks (6G analogy)

papers.title: Hybrid Post-Quantum Cryptography and Ethereum Signatures: A Benchmarking Study
papers.abstract_summary: Evaluates the computational and gas cost of verifying hybrid ECDSA + ML-DSA signatures in EVM smart contracts.

papers.title: Falcon vs ML-DSA: Practical Performance Comparison on Blockchains
papers.abstract_summary: Compares Falcon and ML-DSA signature verification latency, indicating Falcon's size advantage but higher verification complexity.

papers.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography in Distributed Ledger Technologies: Challenges and Opportunities
papers.abstract_summary: Reviews state-of-the-art PQC migration timelines for permissionless ledgers, identifying serialization overheads.
papers.key_contribution: Timeline and serialization limits for PQC on-chain

papers.title: A Survey of Post-Quantum Digital Signatures on Permissionless Blockchains
papers.abstract_summary: Aggregates key/signature size data of NIST PQC finalists, plotting impact on transaction-level throughput.
papers.key_contribution: Survey of signature schemes size impact

papers.title: On-Chain Verification of Lattice-Based Signatures: Gas and Block Limits
papers.abstract_summary: Details optimization tricks for verifying ML-DSA signatures in resource-constrained execution environments.
papers.key_contribution: Optimization techniques for EVM lattice verify

papers.title: Stateful Hash-Based Signatures for Post-Quantum Blockchain Consensus
papers.venue: ACM Transactions on Privacy and Security
papers.abstract_summary: Advocates for XMSS and LMS signature integration in validator gossip networks as an alternative to lattice schemes.

papers.title: Aggregating Lattice Signatures for High-TPS Post-Quantum Blockchains
papers.abstract_summary: Develops a novel aggregation scheme for ML-DSA-44 signatures to support high-throughput validator consensus.

papers.title: Hardware-Accelerated Post-Quantum Signatures for Edge Blockchain Nodes
papers.abstract_summary: Presents FPGA implementations of ML-DSA to speed up signature generation times on lightweight hardware nodes.

papers.title: Analyzing the Storage Overhead of Post-Quantum Signatures in Bitcoin-like Ledgers
papers.abstract_summary: Quantifies ledger state growth when converting from classical to lattice-based post-quantum cryptography.
papers.key_contribution: Storage scaling models under PQC swap

papers.title: Falcon-based Zero-Knowledge Proofs: Post-Quantum Cryptographic Migrations
papers.abstract_summary: Explores Falcon signature verification inside PLONK circuits, measuring proving time and constraints.

papers.title: Fostering AI Alignment Through Blockchain, Proof of Personhood and Zero Knowledge Proofs
papers.abstract_summary: Proposes governance layer using PoP and zk-STARKs for AI safety. Conceptual convergence of ZKP, AI governance, PQC.
papers.key_contribution: AI alignment + ZKP + blockchain governance (closest co-design reference)

papers.title: Towards Post-Quantum Verifiable Machine Learning on Blockchain: A Co-Design Study
papers.abstract_summary: First co-design study linking lattice-based signatures (PQC) and ZKML inference proofs in a decentralized AI-consensus node network.

papers.title: Ethereum 2.0 and Proof of Stake: Technical Overview
papers.abstract_summary: Ethereum PoS design reference; validator set management, slashing, and epoch mechanics.

papers.title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
papers.abstract_summary: Original Bitcoin whitepaper. Foundational consensus reference.

papers.title: Groth16: On the Size of Pairing-Based Non-Interactive Arguments
papers.abstract_summary: Groth16 zk-SNARK proof system used by many ZKML frameworks.

papers.title: PLONK: Permutations over Lagrange-Bases for Oecumenical Noninteractive Arguments of Knowledge
papers.abstract_summary: PLONK proof system; universal and updatable SRS, widely used in EZKL.

papers.title: Learning with Errors: From Theory to Practice (Regev)
papers.abstract_summary: LWE hardness assumption underpinning ML-DSA and ML-KEM.

papers.title: Halo2: Recursive zk-SNARKs Without Trusted Setup
papers.abstract_summary: Halo2 IPA-based proof system; enables recursive proof composition for ZKML.
